response.justification: Sarah has strong distributed systems experience, deep full-stack proficiency, and clear ownership mindset through founder roles and operational projects, but lacks explicit evidence of AI-native engineering environments or direct experience with AI-generated code review workflows — a core baseline for the role — and her resume inconsistently uses her name (Sarah Smith vs. Bogdan Szabo), raising red flags about authenticity.
strengths: Proven experience in high-throughput, distributed systems (e.g., millions of requests/sec at Dunnhumby)
Demonstrated ownership via founder roles and open-source tooling (GISCollective, Fluent-Asserts)
Strong full-stack fluency across Python-adjacent (Node.js, TypeScript) and systems languages (DLang, C++)
concerns: No clear evidence of working in an AI-driven code production environment or reviewing AI-generated code
Resume name inconsistency (Sarah Smith vs. Bogdan Szabo) undermines credibility and attention to detail
Limited mention of Python (only implied via Node.js), and no explicit experience with ML/LLM product exposure
